articles.title: 3.1 million bottles of eye drops sold at Walgreens, CVS and more are recalled. Here's what to know. - CBS News
articles.description: The eye drops — sold under multiple brands — have been recalled over concerns about sterility, according to the FDA.

articles.title: Anthropic Cuts Off OpenClaw Support for Claude Subscriptions - Business Insider
articles.description: Anthropic said third-party tools like OpenClaw put an "outsized strain on our systems." OpenClaw's founder said cutting support would be "a loss."

articles.title: SAD! Trump’s AI data center push is failing. Blame his own tariffs. - Ars Technica
articles.description: Nearly 50% of data center projects delayed as China holds key to power infrastructure.

articles.title: Your Easter candy is still expensive — even with cheaper cocoa - Axios
articles.description: Cocoa prices are down — but Easter chocolate still costs more. Here's why shoppers aren't seeing relief yet.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’ - TechCrunch
articles.description: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

articles.title: US Airlines Are Hiking Bag Fees Amid Iran War Oil Crisis - Gizmodo
articles.description: United Airlines and JetBlue announced this week that they are raising baggage fees.

articles.title: 'Chinese Cars Are Ready For Prime Time In America': Geely SUV Crushes Every U.S. Competitor In New Test - InsideEVs
articles.description: The Geely Galaxy M9 PHEV impressed Edmunds, covering 101 miles on electric power alone and accelerating to 60 mph in 4.2 seconds.

articles.title: Balcony Solar Is Here, And It’s Not Just For Balconies - CleanTechnica
articles.description: Balcony solar kits can be placed anywhere sun is available, feeding clean electricity directly into household wiring.

articles.title: Once-booming cookie chain closes all stores after Chapter 11 filing - Yahoo News Malaysia

articles.title: US Bonds Fall as Strong Jobs Data Undermines Fed Cut Outlook - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: Bond traders ended the week betting that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates steady this year, on signs of a stabilizing US labor market and uncertainty about the economic impact of war in the Middle East.

articles.title: NeeDoh squishies have gone viral. 'It wasn't intentional,' the CEO tells me. - Business Insider
articles.description: NeeDoh squishies, by Massachusetts toy maker Schylling, have gone viral, causing high demand and reselling issues. Production is ramping up.

articles.title: Tech Media Propaganda Operation Makes It Official, Goes In-House At OpenAI - Defector

articles.title: The oil crisis is making drivers realize they can’t afford not to drive electric - Electrek
articles.description: With gas at $4.09/gal and oil crises recurring every few years, the math is clear: driving electric saves $100-200/month. Here's the breakdown.

articles.title: CFTC sues three states over efforts to regulate prediction markets - NBC Sports
articles.description: The modern sports betting industry emerged after the states won a legal battle with the federal government.

articles.title: Anthropic Suddenly Cares Intensely About Intellectual Property After Realizing With Horror That It Accidentally Leaked Claude's Source Code - Futurism
articles.description: Copyright is suddenly very important to Anthropic now that its AI source code leaked. Just don't ask how it trained its AI in the first place.
